name: Tyche Extended
description: Tyche, in Greek mythology, is the goddess of fortune, chance, providence, and fate. She is often depicted wearing a mural crown, representing the walls of a city, and carrying a cornucopia, symbolizing abundance and prosperity. Tyche's influence is pervasive, affecting not only individuals but also entire cities and nations. Her power is unpredictable and capricious, capable of bestowing great fortune or inflicting devastating misfortune without warning. While often associated with positive outcomes, Tyche embodies the inherent uncertainty and unpredictability of life. She is not necessarily benevolent or malevolent; rather, she represents the impartial forces of chance that shape human destiny. Her worship was particularly popular during the Hellenistic period, as cities sought her favor to ensure their continued success and prosperity. Temples dedicated to Tyche were common, and she was often depicted on coins and other artifacts. Tyche's Roman counterpart is Fortuna. In some accounts, she is considered one of the Oceanids, daughters of Oceanus and Tethys. Extended interpretations portray Tyche as influencing not just material fortune but also intellectual and artistic endeavors, suggesting that even inspiration and creativity are subject to the whims of chance. She is a complex figure, embodying both the promise of prosperity and the threat of sudden ruin, a constant reminder of the precariousness of human existence.
subtitle: Goddess of Fortune, Chance, and Fate

keyMyths.title: The Founding of Alexandria
keyMyths.description: It is said that Tyche favored the founding of Alexandria, bestowing upon it immense wealth and prosperity due to its strategic location and trade opportunities.

keyMyths.title: The Fall of a City
keyMyths.description: A once-thriving city, arrogant in its success, neglected to honor Tyche and was subsequently plagued by misfortune, leading to its decline and eventual ruin.

keyMyths.title: A Merchant's Gamble
keyMyths.description: A humble merchant, guided by a dream attributed to Tyche, took a daring risk that yielded him immense wealth, proving that fortune favors the bold, but is ultimately governed by chance.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Mentions the lineage and family of Tyche.
sources.text: Mentions the lineage and family of Tyche.

sources.author: Pausanias
sources.description: Describes temples and statues dedicated to Tyche in various Greek cities.
sources.text: Describes temples and statues dedicated to Tyche in various Greek cities.

sources.author: Diodorus Siculus
sources.description: Provides historical context and mentions Tyche's role in the prosperity of cities.
